mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, and PKC to block proliferative signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as PKC412; approved for FLT3‑mutant AML; active in FLT3‑ITD lines including MOLM‑13

mechanism: Potently and selectively inhibits FLT3 and AXL, suppressing FLT3‑driven survival signaling
additional_notes: Also known as ASP2215; approved for relapsed/refractory FLT3‑mutant AML; very low‑nM IC50 in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, RAF, VEGFR, and PDGFR, reducing proliferation and angiogenic signaling
additional_notes: Used off‑label in FLT3‑ITD AML; active in MOLM‑13

mechanism: Highly potent FLT3 tyrosine kinase inhibitor, blocking FLT3‑ITD–driven signaling
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; strong activity in FLT3‑ITD AML lines

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3, KIT, PDGFR, and VEGFR, impairing growth and survival signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Shows anti‑proliferative effects in AML cell lines

mechanism: Selectively inhibits anti‑apoptotic protein BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑199; approved in AML combination regimens; AML IC50 often sub‑100 nM; synergy with hypomethylating agents

mechanism: Inhibits anti‑apoptotic proteins BCL‑2 and BCL‑XL to trigger apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as ABT‑263; extensive preclinical AML data

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA/RNA and inhibits DNMTs, causing DNA hypomethylation and antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Also known as 5‑AZA; approved for MDS/AML; often requires 72–120 h exposure for robust effects

mechanism: Incorporates into DNA and traps DNMTs, leading to DNA hypomethylation and re‑expression of silenced genes
additional_notes: Also known as 5‑aza‑2′‑deoxycytidine; approved for AML/MDS; often needs ≥72 h exposure

mechanism: Inhibits class I and II histone deacetylases, altering chromatin structure and inducing differentiation and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; shows differentiation and pro‑apoptotic effects in AML cells

mechanism: Potently inhibits multiple HDAC isoforms, leading to accumulation of acetylated histones and apoptosis
additional_notes: Also known as LBH589; strong preclinical activity in AML models

mechanism: Inhibits mutant IDH1, reducing oncometabolite 2‑HG production and promoting differentiation of leukemic cells
additional_notes: Also known as AG‑120; approved in IDH1‑mutated AML; can serve as comparator/negative control in IDH1‑WT MOLM‑13

mechanism: Inhibits mutant IDH2, decreasing 2‑HG and enabling differentiation of AML blasts
additional_notes: Also known as AG‑221; approved in IDH2‑mutated AML; can be used as comparator in IDH2‑WT contexts

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2, blocking cytokine‑dependent JAK‑STAT survival signaling
additional_notes: Can suppress cytokine‑dependent survival in some AML settings and stromal co‑culture models

mechanism: Allosterically inhibits mTORC1, reducing protein synthesis and growth signaling in AML cells
additional_notes: Also known as RAD001; mTOR signaling often activated in AML, including FLT3‑ITD lines

mechanism: Inhibits MEK1 and MEK2, blocking downstream ERK signaling in the RAS/MAPK pathway
additional_notes: Shows in‑vitro anti‑proliferative effects in various leukemias; RAS/MAPK frequently active in AML

mechanism: Reversibly inhibits the 26S proteasome, leading to accumulation of misfolded proteins and apoptosis
additional_notes: Induces apoptosis in AML cells and can sensitize to other agents

mechanism: Irreversibly inhibits the proteasome’s chymotrypsin‑like activity, triggering proteotoxic stress and cell death
additional_notes: Second‑generation proteasome inhibitor; strong cytotoxicity in AML lines in vitro

mechanism: Binds to HSP90 and inhibits its chaperone function, leading to degradation of client oncoproteins such as FLT3
additional_notes: Also known as Tanespimycin; closely related analogue 17‑DMAG shares mechanism; destabilizes FLT3 and other oncogenic kinases in AML including MOLM‑13

category: Retinoic acid receptor agonist / differentiation agent
mechanism: Activates nuclear retinoic acid receptors (RARs), promoting differentiation and reduced proliferation of leukemic blasts
additional_notes: Commonly abbreviated ATRA; standard therapy in APL; can exert differentiation and anti‑proliferative effects in some non‑APL AML lines, often in combination regimens
